zh.siteSubtitle: 美国文豪与未来预见者（1835-1910）
zh.homeIntro1: 马克·吐温（Mark Twain，1835年11月30日—1910年4月21日），本名塞缪尔·朗霍恩·克莱门斯，是美国最伟大的作家、幽默家和社会评论家之一。他以《汤姆·索亚历险记》和《哈克贝利·费恩历险记》等作品闻名于世，被威廉·福克纳称为「美国文学之父」。
zh.homeIntro2: 除了文学成就，吐温还展现了惊人的预见力。他在1898年的小说中描述了一种连接全球的信息网络——远距电视镜（Telelectroscope），精确预见了互联网的诞生。他预言自己将随哈雷彗星而去，最终果然在彗星回归时离世。他对帝国主义、媒体操纵、宗教伪善和人类愚行的辛辣观察，在一个多世纪后依然切中时弊。
zh.homeIntro3: 吐温的「预言」并非神秘主义，而是源于他对人性和社会的深刻洞察。他用讽刺和幽默揭示了人类社会运作的底层逻辑，这些观察因触及本质而历久弥新。本站整理了他最具预见性的观点，并对照现实进行了验证。
keyPointsList: 1835年 —— 马克·吐温出生，恰逢哈雷彗星造访地球
1889年 —— 出版《亚瑟王宫廷里的康涅狄格州扬基人》，讽刺技术崇拜
1898年 —— 发表短篇《1904年伦敦泰晤士报》，描述全球信息网络
1901年 —— 发表反帝国主义演说《致坐在黑暗中的人》
1909年 —— 预言自己将随哈雷彗星离世
1910年 —— 哈雷彗星回归次日，吐温逝世，预言应验
sourceList: 预言内容整理自马克·吐温的公开出版物、书信和演讲记录（Wikipedia）
《1904年伦敦泰晤士报》原文可查阅吐温作品集（Project Gutenberg）
哈雷彗星与吐温生卒的关联已被广泛记录（History.com via Archive.org）
验证评价基于公开新闻报道、历史记录和统计数据
编者观点不代表学术定论
站点图标采用哈雷彗星符号——吐温生于彗星来临之年，逝于彗星回归之日，这是他最传奇的预言

en.homeIntro1: Mark Twain (November 30, 1835 – April 21, 1910), born Samuel Langhorne Clemens, was one of America's greatest writers, humorists, and social critics. Famous for The Adventures of Tom Sawyer and Adventures of Huckleberry Finn, he was called 'the father of American literature' by William Faulkner.
en.homeIntro2: Beyond his literary achievements, Twain displayed remarkable prescience. In an 1898 short story, he described a global information network called the 'Telelectroscope' — an uncanny prefiguration of the internet. He predicted his own death would coincide with Halley's Comet, and indeed died the day after the comet's closest approach. His sharp observations on imperialism, media manipulation, religious hypocrisy, and human folly remain strikingly relevant over a century later.
en.homeIntro3: Twain's 'prophecies' were not mystical but rooted in his profound understanding of human nature and society. Through satire and humor, he exposed the underlying logic of how societies operate — observations that endure because they touch on fundamentals. This site compiles his most prescient insights and verifies them against reality.
en.coreQuote: "History doesn't repeat itself, but it often rhymes."
keyPointsList: 1835 —— Mark Twain born during Halley's Comet's visit to Earth
1889 —— Published A Connecticut Yankee in King Arthur's Court, satirizing tech worship
1898 —— Published 'From the London Times of 1904', describing a global info network
1901 —— Delivered anti-imperialist essay 'To the Person Sitting in Darkness'
1909 —— Predicted he would depart with Halley's Comet
1910 —— Died the day after Halley's Comet's closest approach, fulfilling his prediction
sourceList: Predictions compiled from Mark Twain's published works, letters, and speeches (Wikipedia)
Original text of 'From the London Times of 1904' available in Twain's collected works (Project Gutenberg)
Halley's Comet connection widely documented (History.com via Archive.org)
Verification based on public news reports, historical records, and statistical data
Editorial opinions do not represent academic consensus
Site icon: Halley's Comet — Twain was born when the comet came, and died when it returned, his most legendary prediction
